articles.title: OpenAI bets on families as ChatGPT goes deeper into households
articles.summary: ChatGPT is hiring a dedicated product manager to build experiences for families, caregivers, and older adults, according to a job posting.

articles.title: Meta removes controversial AI feature on Instagram after backlash
articles.summary: "Our intent was to provide a useful creative tool and to give people control over whether their public content could be referenced in this way," the company said in a blog post. "We've heard the feedback that this feature missed the mark, so it's no longer available."

articles.title: SK Hynix raises $26.5B in the biggest foreign IPO in US history, is urged to build new US fabs
articles.summary: The AI chip boom just produced its biggest Wall Street moment yet. Now SK Hynix and Samsung are being asked to build U.S. factories.

articles.title: OpenAI launches its new family of models with GPT-5.6
articles.summary: OpenAI's latest family of models promises improvements across a range of areas, including cybersecurity.

articles.title: An AI agent startup just let its agent run its $100M fundraise
articles.summary: Lyzr, a startup that builds AI agents for enterprises, used its own AI agent to raise a $100 million round — proof, evidently, that the product actually works.

articles.title: OpenAI is shutting down Atlas, but its AI browser ambitions are still growing
articles.summary: OpenAI is sunsetting its AI-powered browser after less than a year. But it's moving some agentic browsing features to its desktop app and a Chrome extension.

articles.summary: Should Anthropic trust Elon Musk to host its models? With about $40 billion in revenue at stake, Musk insists that the company can.

articles.title: Can AI answer the $3 trillion question?

articles.title: Meta enters the crowded AI coding battle with Muse Spark 1.1
articles.summary: Meta's pitch to users is Spark's ability to handle large agentic workloads, fix bugs, and help with large code migrations — the kind of automation that enterprises are increasingly turning to AI companies to provide.

articles.title: New York Times says OpenAI hid evidence in ChatGPT copyright trial
articles.summary: News publishers say OpenAI hid tools and datasets that could identify copyrighted journalism in ChatGPT outputs, escalating their lawsuit with a new motion for sanctions.

articles.title: Google will now disclose which ads are made with AI
articles.summary: While Google prohibits misleading and deceptive ads, an ad can still leverage AI to create some type of synthetic or digitally altered content. Until now, that's something Google only required election ads to disclose.

articles.summary: The company is using the cash to open an office in the Bay Area and compete for talent there, "strengthening its position at the heart of the world's leading AI ecosystem."

articles.title: Instagram users: Here’s how to stop Meta’s AI from using your photos
articles.summary: Muse Image allows users to generate AI images using photos from public Instagram accounts. As long as a person's profile is public, another user can tag that account and use their images as part of an AI-generated creation.
